div.h2: Signal Re-injection vs Suppression: Tracer Decision Flow

p.em: Real SIGTERM intercepted at syscall boundary — re-inject or suppress?

ul.li: SIGTRAP-based stops (syscall, event, exec) are ptrace artifacts — no signal to deliver → pass 0
All other WSTOPSIG values are real signals intercepted before delivery → pass stop_sig
Passing 0 for a real signal = silent suppression (SIGTERM, SIGCHLD, SIGALRM all break the program)
Passing stop_sig for a ptrace artifact = spurious signal injection (delivers a SIGTRAP the program did not expect)

g.text: ④-B SUPPRESS PATH (BUG — do not do this)
⑤ Stop-type decision table: re-inject vs suppress

text.tspan: SIGTRAP | 0x80 (syscall stop) → ptrace(PTRACE_SYSCALL, pid, 0, 0 ) NO signal
ANY OTHER sig (real signal) → ptrace(PTRACE_SYSCALL, pid, 0, sig) RE-INJECT
